title: A Casual Conversation with James M. Hewitt: LARPing! Warhammer Quest! Giant Tables! And More!
body:
Daniel (@Dungeondive) chats with game designer James M. Hewitt, exploring a range of fascinating topics related to tabletop game design and the creative process behind some iconic games.

James discusses his journey at Games Workshop, where he worked on major titles like Warhammer Quest: Silver Tower and helped shape the evolution of dungeon crawlers. He delves into the challenges of translating complex stories and world-building into streamlined, accessible gameplay, balancing mechanics with narrative immersion, and ensuring that players feel truly invested in the experience.

The conversation also touches on James’ work with Mantic Games, including the development of Hellboy: The Board Game, where he shares how his love for the comics inspired unique design choices that blend investigation, action, and storytelling. James and Daniel also dive into the broader world of tabletop gaming, discussing everything from the importance of creating games that are easy to set up and play, to the growing trend of indie RPGs and their influence on the design landscape.

Needy Cat Games

Needy Cat Games

Tabletop games design company focusing on board games, card games, roleplaying games, wargames and miniatures games. Design, review, consultation for the tabletop games industry.
